businessSummary.oneLiner: Government and enterprise AI data analytics platform leader, operating Gotham (defense), Foundry (enterprise), and AIP (AI platform)
businessSummary.description: Palantir builds AI data analytics platforms for government and enterprise customers. FY2025 revenue hit $4.48B (+60%). U.S. commercial revenue surpassed $650M (Q4 +137% YoY). The Pentagon adopted Maven AI as an official program of record, deploying it across all branches. Palantir was selected as the software developer for the $185B Golden Dome missile defense project. Its tools are actively used in the Iran conflict, and CNBC called it 'defense tech's opportunity.'
howTheyMakeMoney: Enterprise AI platform (Foundry) - large-scale data integration
AIP (AI Platform) - generative AI integration platform
AIP Bootcamps - rapid customer onboarding programs

keyMetrics.name: Trailing P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: 6.3x the S&P 500 IT sector average of 34x. 4.8x the software sector average of 45x. Still extreme premium despite further decline

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E (FY2026E)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026 EPS estimate of $1.22. Still 3x the growth stock average of 30-40x, though slightly compressed by the drop

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (FY2026E)
keyMetrics.interpretation: From FY2025 $4.48B to FY2026 $7.40B projected. Iran war expansion could further accelerate AI defense demand

keyMetrics.name: P/S Ratio
keyMetrics.interpretation: 5-7x the software sector average of 10-15x. Slightly compressed from the drop

keyMetrics.name: YTD Performance
keyMetrics.interpretation: Down 22% year-to-date. Down 33.7% from the 52-week high of $207.52. Dropped 3.85% on March 30 alone

keyMetrics.name: RSI
keyMetrics.interpretation: Approaching oversold territory (30). RSI has declined sharply over 4 consecutive down days. Technical bounce possible

growthDrivers.title: Stellantis 5-Year Partnership Renewal and Expansion (3/30)
growthDrivers.description: Palantir renewed and expanded its partnership with Stellantis, the world's 4th largest automaker. A flagship case for Foundry-powered manufacturing AI, proving enterprise customer expansion

growthDrivers.title: CNBC: 'Iran War Is Defense Tech's Opportunity, Palantir Tools in Active Use' (3/28)
growthDrivers.description: CNBC analyzed the Iran war as an opportunity for defense tech companies. Palantir has secured billions in Pentagon contracts, with CEO Karp confirming active battlefield use. Houthi involvement could drive further demand

growthDrivers.title: Motley Fool: 'Palantir Blockbuster Week' 3 Key Developments (3/29)
growthDrivers.description: Motley Fool highlighted Golden Dome, Maven official adoption, and Polymarket partnership as 'the three pillars of a blockbuster week.' Continued fundamental strengthening

growthDrivers.title: Golden Dome $185B Missile Defense Software Selection (3/25)
growthDrivers.description: Palantir and Anduril selected to co-develop software for Trump administration's $185B Golden Dome missile defense system. Urgency amplified by Houthi missile attacks

growthDrivers.title: Polymarket AI Anti-Fraud Partnership (3/26)
growthDrivers.description: Palantir's Vergence AI handles real-time trade surveillance and user behavior monitoring for Polymarket. Motley Fool called it 'the sports betting watchdog.' Entry point into financial services

growthDrivers.title: Maven AI Official Military-Wide Adoption (Program of Record, 3/23)
growthDrivers.description: Pentagon designated Maven Smart System as an official program of record, confirming long-term deployment across all military branches. Battle-tested in the Iran conflict

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 20+ years of government defense AI track record, top-level security clearances, Maven military-wide adoption, Iran war battlefield validation. Moats that money cannot buy

moats.description: Deeply integrated Ontology platform in government/military systems. Replacement costs are prohibitively high once deployed
Top-level security clearances and 20+ years of government trust. Virtually impossible for new entrants to replicate
Real-time targeting in the Iran conflict. Expanding frontlines with Houthi involvement drives additional demand
Rapid customer acquisition through AIP Bootcamps and expanding Ontology data model ecosystem

competitors.name: Anduril Industries
competitors.detail: Defense AI/drone startup. Collaborates with Palantir on Golden Dome. $60B valuation

competitors.name: Snowflake / Databricks
competitors.detail: Enterprise data platform competitors. Significantly behind on government/defense access

competitors.name: Microsoft / AWS
competitors.detail: Cloud giants entering government AI. Cannot match Ontology-level deep integration

competitors.name: C3.ai
competitors.detail: Enterprise AI platform. Far behind in both revenue scale and growth rate

recentNews.headline: 247WallSt: 'Palantir Drops 4%: Can AI Partnerships Justify One of the Market's Most Expensive Valuations?'
recentNews.significance: 247WallSt analyzed the 4% drop: 'Today's decline is less about Palantir's business and more about the price investors are willing to pay in a high-fear, risk-off environment.' Watch for $140 and $145 reclamation. YTD -22%

recentNews.headline: Parameter.io: PLTR Falls 3.85% to $137.55 Despite 5-Year Stellantis AI Deal Expansion
recentNews.significance: Despite the Stellantis partnership renewal, shares fell 3.85% and dropped another 0.81% after hours. Macro selling pressure overwhelmed the positive catalyst

recentNews.headline: Barchart: 'Palantir Is Developing the Core of the Golden Dome. Does That Make PLTR Stock a Buy?'
recentNews.significance: Mizuho Securities reaffirmed Outperform rating with $195 target. Revenue growth acceleration, margin expansion outlook. Golden Dome could reshape long-term defense structure

recentNews.headline: TradingKey: PLTR Down 3.81%, Double Pressure from Insider Selling and Macro Headwinds
recentNews.significance: Peter Thiel and executives continued selling throughout March 2026, weighing on investor sentiment. Potential internal concerns about near-term growth or current valuation

recentNews.headline: Stellantis 5-Year Partnership Renewal and Expansion Announced
recentNews.significance: Long-term partnership renewal with the world's 4th largest automaker. Proven success of Foundry-based manufacturing AI

analystOpinions.notableComment: Mizuho $195 Outperform reaffirmed. MarketBeat consensus $198.59 (+44.4%). 247WallSt: 'Not about the business, about macro fear'

risks.title: Down 3.85% on catalyst day, macro selloff overwhelming fundamentals
risks.description: Fell 3.85% on the day of Stellantis 5-year deal announcement, dropped another 0.81% after hours. 247WallSt: 'less about Palantir's business, more about the price in a high-fear, risk-off environment.' Positive catalysts failing to defend the stock

risks.title: Extreme valuation, P/E 215x
risks.description: P/E 215x (6.3x IT sector average of 34x). Motley Fool calculated earnings need to grow 330%+ to bring P/E down to 50x. Even war benefits are already priced in

risks.title: Oil at $100, macro tech selloff
risks.description: Houthi involvement, oil above $100, inflation reignition fears. Selling pressure on high-valuation tech stocks outweighs defense tailwinds

risks.title: Peter Thiel and executive continuous insider selling in March
risks.description: TradingKey reports persistent insider selling throughout March. Seeking Alpha counters with 10b5-1 plan argument, but sentiment impact is negative

risks.title: Below both 50-day and 200-day SMA, YTD -22%
risks.description: Below SMA50 ($149) and SMA200 ($169). Down 3.85% on 3/30, deepening technical weakness. Support at $126 is wide open

risks.title: Divided analyst opinions
risks.description: 17 Buy, 14 Hold, 5 Sell. Market skepticism on valuation persists

buyReasons.title: Stellantis 5-Year Renewal + Iran War: Dual Defense/Enterprise Tailwinds
buyReasons.rationale: Stellantis 5-year partnership renewal (enterprise) plus active battlefield use per CNBC (defense). Demand growing on both fronts. Golden Dome missile defense urgency rising

buyReasons.title: 247WallSt: 'Decline Is About Macro Fear, Not the Business'
buyReasons.rationale: The drop is driven by risk-off environment, not fundamental deterioration. Blockbuster week catalysts remain unreflected in the stock. Potential snap-back when macro stabilizes

buyReasons.title: Down 34% from highs, RSI 38 approaching oversold
buyReasons.rationale: Down 34% from 52-week high of $207. RSI 38 approaching oversold territory. Technical bounce probability increasing

buyReasons.title: Consensus $199 (+44%), Mizuho $195 Outperform Reaffirmed
buyReasons.rationale: Barchart highlighted Golden Dome Core role as Mizuho reaffirmed $195. The drop has expanded upside from 37.7% to 44.4%

overallOpinion: Palantir fell 3.85% to $137.55 on the same day it announced its Stellantis 5-year partnership renewal. 247WallSt concluded: 'Today's decline is less about Palantir's business and more about the price investors are willing to pay in a high-fear, risk-off environment.' Down another 0.81% after hours

P/E at 215x (4.8x the software sector average). Thiel and executives sold throughout March, though Seeking Alpha notes it's a pre-scheduled 10b5-1 plan. Barchart highlighted Golden Dome Core development as Mizuho reaffirmed $195 Outperform

Below SMA50 ($149) and SMA200 ($169). YTD -22%, down 34% from peak. RSI 38 approaching oversold. Consensus $199 (+44%). Fundamentals are the strongest ever, but macro is overwhelming. Hold, scale in after macro stabilizes

investmentVerdict.summary: The 3.85% drop despite Stellantis 5-year deal shows macro selloff's power. Golden Dome Core + Maven + Stellantis make fundamentals the strongest ever, but P/E 215x and oil at $100 cap near-term upside. Down 34% from peak with RSI 38 approaching oversold, making valuation more attractive. Hold, scale in after macro stabilizes
